sections.title: OpenAI API Basics and Text Generation
key_points: OpenAI API can generate various text types: code, math equations, JSON, prose
Simple example using Responses API with gpt-5.4 model
Response structure contains output array with message objects
output_text property aggregates all text outputs for convenience
Structured Outputs feature for JSON format generation

sections.title: Model Selection Guidelines
key_points: Reasoning models: internal chain of thought, excel at complex tasks, slower/expensive
GPT models: fast, cost-efficient, intelligent, need explicit instructions
Large vs small models: trade-offs between speed, cost, and intelligence
gpt-4.1 recommended as balanced option for intelligence, speed, cost

sections.title: Prompt Engineering Fundamentals
key_points: Process of writing effective instructions for consistent desired output
Mix of art and science due to non-deterministic nature
Pin production apps to specific model snapshots for consistency
Build evals to measure prompt performance and monitor iterations
Different model types require different prompting approaches

sections.title: Message Roles and Instructions
key_points: Instructions parameter: high-level behavior guidance, takes priority
Three message roles: developer (highest priority), user, assistant
Developer messages: system rules and business logic
User messages: inputs and configuration
Instructions only apply to current request, not conversation history

sections.title: Reusable Prompts and Templates
key_points: Create reusable prompts in OpenAI dashboard with placeholders
Use prompt parameter with id, version, and variables properties
Variables can be strings or other input types (images, files)
Enables prompt iteration without changing integration code

sections.title: Message Formatting Best Practices
key_points: Use Markdown headers/lists for structure and hierarchy
XML tags to delineate content boundaries and add metadata
Developer message structure: Identity → Instructions → Examples → Context
Position frequently reused content early for prompt caching benefits

sections.title: Advanced Techniques
key_points: Few-shot learning: include input/output examples to steer model behavior
Show diverse range of examples for pattern recognition
Include relevant context for proprietary data or constraints
RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) for external data integration
Context window planning: models have token limits (100k to 1M tokens)

sections.title: Model-Specific Prompting Strategies
key_points: GPT models (gpt-5): need precise, explicit instructions with logic and data
Reasoning models: work better with high-level guidance, like senior coworker
GPT models: like junior coworker, need explicit step-by-step instructions
Different approaches needed for coding, front-end, and agentic tasks

summary.title: Comprehensive Guide to OpenAI API Prompt Engineering Techniques
takeaways: Prompt engineering is both art and science requiring systematic approach with evals and model pinning
Different model types (reasoning vs GPT) require fundamentally different prompting strategies
Message roles create hierarchy: developer messages override user messages for consistent behavior
Structured formatting with Markdown and XML improves model understanding and response quality
Few-shot learning with diverse examples can steer model behavior without fine-tuning
Context window management and prompt caching optimization are crucial for cost and performance
concepts: Context window limitations and token management
Structured outputs and JSON schema compliance
actionable: Pin production applications to specific model snapshots for consistency
Build evaluation systems to measure prompt performance across iterations
Structure developer messages with Identity → Instructions → Examples → Context format
Place frequently reused content early in prompts for caching benefits
Use diverse examples in few-shot learning to improve pattern recognition
Choose appropriate model type based on task complexity and instruction detail needs
Implement reusable prompt templates in dashboard for easier iteration
